free_text: Study of the 89Y(a,a)89Y reaction close to the
Coulomb barrier
revised data tabulated
preliminary data
Evaporated Yttrium onto carbon foil.
The thickness of the targets was determined by
measuring the energy loss of alpha particles from a
mixed alpha source and found roughly 200 ug/cm2.
Angular distributions were measured with two pairs of
silicon detectors (with an angular separation of 10
degree between each pair of detectors).
The detectors were rotated around the target
covering an angular range between 20 and 170 degrees.
The distance between the detectors and the target was
20 cm.
Two additional detectors were used to monitor the beam
position on the target and also to normalize the
measured cross section to the Rutherford cross
section. The thickness of these detectors was 0.5 mm
and the distance from the target was 35 cm.
Statistical uncertainties were between 0.1%
and 4%.
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model code.
Revised entry approved by P.Mohr (2013-11-24)
